rationale: Educ. Code §7.0561 governs the Texas High Performance Schools Consortium's membership, purpose, and reporting. It contains no provision referencing the homestead exemption amount or Tax Code §11.13(b), so increasing the exemption does not conflict with this statute.
quote: The Texas High Performance Schools Consortium is established to inform the governor, legislature, State Board of Education, and commissioner concerning methods for transforming public schools in this state by improving student learning through the development of innovative, next-generation learning standards and assessment and accountability systems
_note: No provision in §7.0561 references homestead exemption amounts, property tax appraised values, or Tax Code §11.13(b). The section deals solely with consortium establishment, membership, and reporting requirements.
